_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how to use commas to separate a dependent clause from an independent clause in a complex sentence.

_source.w: We are learning how to use commas to separate a dependent clause from an independent clause in a complex sentence.
y: Correct comma placement in complex sentences makes our writing clearer and more professional.
This is one of the most practical punctuation skills for the increasingly complex writing we do.
Understanding this convention helps us read complex sentences more fluently.
eg: We use a comma when the dependent clause comes first — 'Although it was late, we kept working.'
We identify that no comma is usually needed when the independent clause comes first — 'We kept working although it was late.'
We find examples in published texts and check the comma placement.
We practise writing complex sentences and placing commas correctly.
We edit a text with missing or misplaced commas in complex sentences.
We create a rule sheet for comma use in complex sentences and use it as a reference.
